meta.purpose: Numeric and structural source of truth for game mechanics. Runtime code should contain the behavioral formulas and read numbers, ids, thresholds, costs, scaling values, multipliers, durations, and prerequisite lists from this file.
authoringRules: Edit this file for balance changes.
Do not generate this file from TypeScript.
Keep formulas in code, but keep their parameters here.
notes: This schema intentionally avoids a rule DSL.
Unlock and visibility logic is represented as lightweight declarative metadata plus prerequisite ids and threshold parameters where practical.
Complex runtime procedures like compliance rollover, max-buy loops, automation cycle stepping, and milestone evaluation still belong in code.

powerOverCap.formulaName: min(1, capacity / usage * overCapRelief) * marketEventMachineEfficiency * energyCompliancePenalty

aggressiveTradingWindow.name: Aggressive Trading Window
aggressiveTradingWindow.description: Temporarily increases human and sector output.

techRally.name: Tech Rally
techRally.description: Growth optimism pushes technology names higher.

energyBoom.name: Energy Boom
energyBoom.description: Energy markets are catching a strong bid.

financialTightening.name: Financial Tightening
financialTightening.description: Risk appetite fades across finance-heavy names.

liquidityCrunch.name: Liquidity Crunch
liquidityCrunch.description: Capital is harder to move and broad output softens.

gridStressWarning.name: Grid Stress Warning
gridStressWarning.description: Machine infrastructure is running under extra strain.

finance.name: Finance
finance.description: Stable baseline market activity for desk-aligned capital.

technology.name: Technology
technology.description: Higher-growth deployment lane with the strongest immediate profit premium.

energy.name: Energy
energy.description: Steady infrastructure-adjacent market with moderate upside and future power synergy.

intern.name: Intern
intern.description: Cheap first hires who only bring in a little cash while the desk gets off the ground.

juniorTrader.name: Junior Trader
juniorTrader.description: The first real trading staff tier, stronger than interns and built to carry the early desk.

seniorTrader.name: Senior Trader
seniorTrader.description: Higher-tier staff with stronger long-term output.

quantTrader.name: Quant Trader
quantTrader.description: The first systematic execution lane, bridging senior human traders into machine-led trading systems.
quantTrader.quirk: displayed as 5 cash/sec baseline, but runtime cash comes from automation cycles

propDesk.name: Prop Desk
propDesk.description: A coordinated in-house team that marks the jump from individual traders to a real desk.

mlTradingBot.description: A heavier model-driven machine tier with longer cycles and larger chunk payouts.

aiTradingBot.name: AI Bot
aiTradingBot.description: Late autonomous systems with the longest cycles, biggest payouts, and the heaviest compute demands.

knownQuirks: premiumDataFeed manual multiplier is applied twice in the current runtime
mandateAlignmentFramework currently has no runtime effect
analyticalModeling currently has no runtime effect
institutionalAccess affects institutional costs but does not effectively soften one overdue penalty path in current runtime
quantTrader display income is average automation value rather than passive tick income
humans and institutions currently use 0 live runtime power despite legacy constants existing
